prompt: The undo-turn feature in our dungeon crawler prototype is haunted and playtesters are rage-quitting. You checkpoint before a boss fight, the fight goes sideways, you roll back — and you're standing on the right floor on the right turn number, but your HP is still whatever the boss left you with, the cursed idol you grabbed mid-fight is still in your bag, and the gold you spent is still gone. So rollback half-works, which is somehow creepier than not working at all. Second thing, might be the same gremlin, might not: on our demo box the engine spins up a fresh GameState per run, and run #2 starts with run #1's leftovers — items, missing HP, rooms already marked visited. All the state juggling lives in dungeon_state.py. python3 test_dungeon_state.py shows both failures. Fix the module and prove it — the test file is off limits.
